English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Robert S. and Eloise C. Wickham papers
Wickham, Robert Sloane, 1877-
Wickham, Eloise Carpenter.
Carpenter, Charles Mayhew.
Carpenter, Alice.
Carpenter, Alvin Rich.
Allaben, Anne E.
DeRidder, Walter.
DeRidder, Catherine S.
8408
3.6 cubic feet. 3.6 cubic feet.
1776-1988. Bound manuscripts, correspondence, photographs, printed material, publications Carpenter-Mayhew papers include correspondence, pamphlets, photographs, legal and estate papers, photographs, and clippings relating to Alvin R. Carpenter, Alice Carpenter, Charles Mayhew, Eloise Carpenter Wickham, and Robert Sloane Wickham. Charles Mayhew Carpenter papers include personal and business correspondence, 1910-1918, including letters from his estranged wife Mary. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 2073 ======= 2073 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 7955035
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Donor
Date completed:
March 2013
EAD encoding:
Julia McDowell, March 2013
Date modified:
Jude Corina, May 2015
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Robert Sloane Wickham was born at Ballston Spa on March 14, 1877. He graduated from Saratoga Springs High School, studied law in the office of Babcock, sperry, and Van Cleve in Binghamton and attended Binghamton High School. In 1897, he won a state scholarship to Cornell and graduated in 1902 with a Bachelor of Law degree. In 1905 he married Eloise Cameron Carpenter. In 1908 they returned to Schuylerville, where he opened a law practice. In 1911, they went back to Binghamton when he was offered a junior partnership with the Chief Counsel of the Excise Department of New York State. He was an avid hiker and wrote two books, Friendly Adirondack Peaks and A Saratoga Boyhood (illustrated by Anne Allaben).
Eloise Cameron Carpenter was born in in Schuylerville, N.Y., the daughter of Alvin Rich Carpenter and Alida Catherine Mayhew. Alida Mayhew was the daughter of Charles Wadsworth Mahew and Maria DeRidder. Maria was the daughter of Walter DeRidder and Alida Vandenburgh. Walter DeRidder was the son of Simon DeRidder, a Brigadier General during the War of 1812 and Maria VAn Schaick. Eloise Carpenter Wickham graduated from Smith College. She had one brother, Charles Mayhew Carpenter who married Mary Alice Hendy of Cynthiana, Ky. They had two children Alvin Rich and Alice. When Charles and Mary Alice divorced Alice stayed with her mother and Charles returned to Binghamton to live with his aunt and uncle. Alvin graduated from Cornell in 1928..
Cite As:
Robert S. and Eloise C. Wickham papers, #8408. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Carpenter-Mayhew papers include correspondence, pamphlets, photographs, legal and estate papers, photographs, and clippings relating to Alvin R. Carpenter, Alice Carpenter, Charles Mayhew, Eloise Carpenter Wickham, and Robert Sloane Wickham. Charles Mayhew Carpenter papers include personal and business correspondence, 1910-1918, including letters from his estranged wife Mary.
Scope and content
Class of 1928 Reunion jacket and hats belonging to Alvin Rich Carpenter.
Scope and content
Oversized items include DeRidder family slave indentures, commissions, officers' roll of Col. Walter DeRidder's Regiment, New York State Militia; Abiel W. Mayhew commission, Columbia Light Infantry, 1809; certificates and diplomas.
Scope and content
Bound volumes include Catherine S. DeRidder travel diary, 1877; Eloise Wickham account books, 1932-1937; Robert S. Wickham physics notebook, undated; album of original drawings by Anne E. Allaben for Robert S. Wickham's book, A Saratoga Boyhood, with final proofs; and book of reviews of A Saratoga Boyhood. Robert Wickham's photograph albums depicting hiking, camping, hunting, and fishing in the Adirondacks, 4 volumes, 1905-1949, with negatives. Another Robert Wickham album depicting family photos, ca. 1860-1956. Also Charles Mayhew Carpenter photographs of family members, houses, and scenes, circa 1900-1914; a carte-de-visit album, probably Alvin and Alida Carpenter; Eloise Wickham school exercises, undated; Regimental record book with orders, muster rolls, and returns, Simon DeRidder, 1812; and various published books and pamphlets.
United States -- History -- War of 1812. Adirondack Mountains (N.Y.) -- Pictorial works. Adirondack Mountains (N.Y.) -- Description and travel. Slavery -- New York (State) Family -- New York (State) -- Schuylerville. Family -- New York (State) -- Saratoga Springs. Family -- New York (State) -- Binghamton. Wickham family. Carpenter family. Mayhew family. DeRidder family. New York State. Militia
7955035 <<<<<<< HEAD Back Log Camp, Sabael, N.Y.1896-195011
Scope and Contents
Pamphlets, 1946-50 and n.d. Includes a history, 1896-1946, a map, 1950 greetings, 1950 general information and rules, and two copies of a much earlier pamphlet, The Back Log Idea.Carpenter, Alice.n.d.12
Scope and Contents
Letter, n.d., to "papa."Carpenter, Alonzo B., Schuylerville.n.d.13
Scope and Contents
Cabinet photograph.Carpenter, Alvin R. I.191414
Scope and Contents
Estate papers, 1914Carpenter, Alvin R. I.191415
Scope and Contents
Letter sent, 1914.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.1914-169516
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914-16 and 1995. Most of the early letters were set by his mother, Mary (Hendy) Carpenter, or his sister, Alice Carpenter, while they were apart due to his parents' separation.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.191717
Scope and Contents
Letters sent, 1917 and n.d. A letter to "Mrs. Landon" written at Oak Street School, 1917, apparently a class exercise; and an undated note to "papa" while he was still with his mother, opening with "I am verry lonesome with out you."Carpenter, Betty, Tulsa, Okla196418
Scope and Contents
Letter sent, 1964, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham; family news.Carpenter family.n.d.19
Scope and Contents
Photograph, unmounted, of Alice and Alvin Carpenter.Carpenter, Mary (Hendy).1914110
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914, on insurance matters.DeRidder family.1905111
Scope and Contents
Genealogical notes; a memo, "Who is Who," by C[atherine] S. DeRidder, Memorial Day, 1905; and a letter, George W. DeRidder, Ballston Spa, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, Binghamton, 15 November 1905.Hendy family.1941112
Scope and Contents
Letter, N.F. McClure, Washington, D.C., to Alvin R. Carpenter, Binghamton, 26 May 1941, enclosing a typescript of a genealogical supplement of the Hendy family of Kentucky.Mayhew, Charles W.1978113
Scope and Contents
Engraving of his residence, Schuylerville, N.Y. It appears, without the full margins, facing p. 260 of Nathaniel B. Sylvester, History of Saratoga County, N.Y. (Philadelphia: Everts and Ensign, 1878).Mayhew, Charles W.1901114
Scope and Contents
Cabinet photograph , inscribed by the sitter to "my dear neice Alice," 1901.Mayhew, Charles W.n.d.115
Scope and Contents
Resolution in memory of CMM, late president, Saratoga Victory Manufacturing Company.Mayhew family.1956-67116
Scope and Contents
Correspondence about the family plot in Prospect Hill Cemetery, Schuylerville, 1956-67, including a plot plan and inscriptions.Saturday Study Club, Schuylerville, N.Y.1901-02117
Scope and Contents
MinutesShakespeare Dramatic Club, Binghamton, N.Y.1956-67118
Scope and Contents
Miscellaneous materials, including "Constitution and By-laws / 19567-1957," along with other materials accumulated by Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, including a newspaper photograph of her as a founding member on its 45th anniversary.Wickham, Alan R.1963-67119
Scope and Contents
Letters sent, 1963-67, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, except for one of 1967 sent to Mr. and Mrs. Alvin R. Carpenter; family news.Wickham, Carolyn.1965120
Scope and Contents
Letter sent to Ruth Everett, 1965.Mayhew family.19011Not Received
Scope and Contents
An issue of The Genealogical Advertiser, vol. 4, no. 1 (March 1901), containing an article, "English Ancestry of Governor Thomas Mayhew."Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).Business papers1947-6521Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.22
Scope and Contents
Cards received.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1966-6723
Scope and Contents
Estate papers, 1966-67. Includes legal papers; an exchange of letters between Conrad and Lucy Meinecke, Springville, N.Y. with Alvin and Betty Carpenter, Binghamton, N,Y, 1967, in reference to Alan Wickham's financial irresponsibility; with a 1961 appraisal of the family residence at 129 Murray Street, including a Polaroid photograph.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).196624
Scope and Contents
"Hospital mail," 1966.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1914-4125
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914-41.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1963-6526
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1963-65.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1951-6627
Scope and Contents
Medical documents, 1951-66.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1946-6428
Scope and Contents
Newspaper clippings, 1946-64, relating to the Monday Afternoon Club, the Shakespeare Dramatic Club and other theatrical events, politics, etc.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1982-8929
Scope and Contents
School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1982-89210
Scope and Contents
School reports, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1901-02211
Scope and Contents
Papers prepared for the Saturday Study Club, Schuylerville, 1901-02Wickham family, Ballston Spa, N.Y.n.d.212
Scope and Contents
Photograph marked "Bob Wickham's baby sister."Wickham, Robert Sloane.1912213
Scope and Contents
"Biographical Sketch of the National Progressive Party's nominee for Broome County Judge and Surrogate." Pamphlet, n.d. [?1912] 2 pp. Contains a biography of the subject along with useful biographical data on his father, Charles H. Wickham.Wickham, Robert Sloane.1917-58214
Scope and Contents
Financial papers, 1917-58; includes record of income, 1917-25; status, 1956; and utility recapitulation, 1957-58.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.215
Scope and Contents
Law licenses.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.216
Scope and Contents
Medical papers.Wickham, Robert Sloane.1893-96217
Scope and Contents
Pre-academic certificates, Saratoga Springs Union School, 1893-96.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.218
Scope and Contents
Woodcraft materials, clipped from newspapers and magazines.Wickham, Robert Sloane and Eloise (Carpenter).1928-70219
Scope and Contents
Ephemera, 1928-70. Includes church bulletins from Trinity Memorial Church (Episcopal), Binghamton; insurance policies; junk mail; and unclassifiable material. An important item is "My constant prayer," in the hand of RSW.Wickham, Robert Sloane and Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.220
Scope and Contents
Photographs.Business191031Personal191132Personal191233Personal191334Business191335Personal191436Business191437Personal191538Business191539Personal1916310Business1916311Personal1952312Businessn.d.313Bankbooks and other recordsn.d.314Allaben Drawingsn.d.41
Scope and Contents
"Original Sketches of Miss Anne E. Allaben for 'A Saratoga Boyhood' by Robert S. Wickham. (Final proofs of all plates made therefrom opposite.)" Scrapbook with pencil drawings and photo prints pasted in. A 2 pp. carbon copy of a review by Arthur C. Parker of Naples, N,Y. [a prominent Seneca and archaeologist retired from the Rochester Museum of Arts and Sciences] is laid in.Adirondack League Club189351
Scope and Contents
Constitution, by-laws, rules, membership list, annual report, list of allotments with surveys and floor plans. No Carpenter or Mayhew is listed, nor any resident of Saratoga Springs; the club was three years old,An Act, to Organize the Militia of the State of New-York, Passed April 21, 1818. Albany: J.W. Clark181852Amendments to the Militia Law, Passed March 13, 1821. Albany: Websters and Skinners, n,d,182153
Scope and Contents
Marked in ink "Simon DeRidder," along with pp. 7-10 of another copy marked "Walter DeRidder."Annual Register of the United States Naval Academy at Annapolis, Md. 1875-76. Washington: Government Printing Office187554Laws of New-York: viz. An Act, to amend an Act, entitled "an Act to organize the Militia of this State" . . . Albany: J. Buel, [1814]181455
Scope and Contents
Incomplete.Official Register of the Officers and Midshipmen of the United States Naval Academy. Newport, Rhode Island. Nov. 1st, 1864. Newport: Frederick A. Pratt186456
Scope and Contents
The list of midshipmen forming the fourth class of 1864 (p. 23) includes Alvin R. Carpenter from "Texas." He initialed inside front cover, and made notes about the fate of his classmates. A pamphlet, "Regulations Governing the Admission of Candidates into the Naval Academy," is inside the front cover.To the Republican Electors of the State of New-York1814?57
Scope and Contents
Pamphlet, uncutThe Book of Common Prayer. London: Eyre and Spottiswoode190058
Scope and Contents
Pocket format. Inscribed on flyleaf: "Robert S. Wickham / Binghamton / N.Y."Wickham, Robert S., Binghamton, N.Y.n.d.59
Scope and Contents
A Saratoga Boyhood, wrapped and tied for mailing.Wickham, Robert S.June 19985
Scope and Contents
Class of 1928 75th Reunion paperweight for Most Cornell DescendantsWickham, Robert S.19985
Scope and Contents
Class of 1928 65th Reunion medalAnsco Company, Binghamton, N.Y.191561
Scope and Contents
Blueprints, two sheets, 1915.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.1928-4262
Scope and Contents
Certificates, 1928-42 and n.d. Includes a swimming certificate from Cornell, diplomas and certifications, and a tribute upon retirement.DeRidder family.1800's63
Scope and Contents
Papers. Included are two slave indentures, 1827; commencement program, Union College, 1818; two commissions, Walter DeRidder, 1818, and another, 1819; "An Oration on Noses," n,d,; several documents on the division of WDR's estate, 1832; letter, E.H. Dixon, House of Refuge, to Jacob Deridder, "Opposite Schuylervile, "1832, on the occasion of Walter's death; deed, Jesse Chatfield to Kilian DeRidder, 1776; officers' roll of Col. Walter DeRidder's Regiment, New York State Militia; and a small receipt for eggs and silk hose.Mayhew, Abiel W.180964
Scope and Contents
Commission, Columbia County Light Infantry, 1809.Schuylerville Standard.191465
Scope and Contents
Issue of June 11, 1914 with front-page obituary of Alvin R, Carpenter; four copies.Wickham, Alan R.192966
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Binghamton Junior High Schools, 1929.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).188967
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Schuylerville Union School, 1889.Wickham, Robert Sloane.189668
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Saratoga Springs Union Free School, 1896.Carpenter, Charles Mayhew.191271
Scope and Contents
Builder's Estimate and Time Book, 1912. Only one week was used, for 11 March 1912 at 1134 West 37 Drive, apparently in greater Los Angeles. Seven workers' hours are recorded including "CMC" and "Hirashima."DeRidder, C[atherine] S.1887-8872
Scope and Contents
Diary of her European travels "with brother" from December 25, 1877 to July 16, 1878. Slipped in are clipped engravings and clippings, apparently of sites visited: Eaton Hall, Hotel and Pension de l'Ours at Grindelwald, Grand Hotel Pension Bellevue au Lac Zurich, Hotel du Nord, Coeln, and Hotel Monet, Vevey.DeRidder, C[atherine] S.188773
Scope and Contents
Partial copy of the previous item.DeRidder, Walter [?].183574
Scope and Contents
Commonplace book, 1835, with transaction entries of a farmer. Difficult to decipher; commodities include eggs, apples, pork, ham; there is a reference to "Troy and Champlain ofice" and another to "wish to draw wood Cambrge."Mayhew, Ida C., Victory Mills, N.Y.186275
Scope and Contents
Exercise book, Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass., 9 October 1862, later used as a clipping book, with newspaper items dated 1862 to 1873. Inside cover is a photograph inscribed "Maplewood Hotel / Pittsfield / Mass / Formerly Maplewood Institute."Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1932-3776
Scope and Contents
Account book, 1932-37, of household and personal expenditures at item level. Inside cover is a membership list of a professional women's organization, the "New York Epsilon Athenians – Binghamton, N.Y."Wickham, Robert S.,n.d.77
Scope and Contents
Physics notebook, n.d., probably used at Saratoga Springs High School, but possibly at Cornell.Wickham, Robert S.194878
Scope and Contents
Book of reviews, letters, etc. of A Saratoga Boyhood (1948), including a short errata (p. 4) and a fine letter (p. 5) from Frances Steloff in which she comments that she was the "official flower girl" in Saratoga until she left for Boston in 1900. A press release and an order blank are slipped in."Aunt Fanny." Night Cap Stories. Boston: Estes and Lauriat189581
Scope and Contents
Inscribed inside front cover: "To Alvin / From / Georgina / Merry Xmas / 1914."An Impartial Narrative of the Most Important Engagements Which Took Place Between His Majesty's Forces and the Rebels, During the Irish Rebellion179882
Scope and Contents
First American Edition. Cambridge, N,Y: Tennery and Stockwell, n.d. Inscribed "Simon DeRidder" on p. 3.Luce. S.B. Seamanship. New York: D. Van Nostrand186383
Scope and Contents
Inscribed in ink on flyleaf: "A.R. Carpenter / Midshipman / U.S.N. / 1864." Covers missingRedfield, Henry S. Civil Procedure. Selected Pleadings. No. I. Cornell University College of Lawn.d.84
Scope and Contents
Inscribed on cover: "Robert Sloane Wickham."Class 1864-1868 / United States Naval Academy / The Fiftieth Anniversary1864-186885, 6
Scope and Contents
Pamphlet, two copies.Album1905-1991
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1917-2392
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1923-38101
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1939-49102
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Karl B Stickley1916111

Scope and Contents
Includes Congress Park, streetcar, overturned railroad car, village from above, and a substantial school building.Carte-de-Visite Albumn.d.171
Scope and Contents
Carte de Visite Album, probably of Alvin R. and Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter of Schuylerville, N.Y. Many photographs were identified on the reverse, and these markings have been copied in pencil to the pages themselves, where there were already some captions. See especially four-year-old girl with rifle (p. 13), man with flute (p. 15), boy in Western Union messenger uniform (p.18). Carpenter family members: Meliza (Rich) Carpenter, Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter, Eloise Carpenter, Alvin R. Carpenter, Frank W. Rich Mayhew family members: Emily W. of Detroit, Portia of Detroit DeRidder family members: J.H. DeRidder of Springfield Friends: "son of Enoch Mudge of Boston"; Walter Edward Houghton (b. 1871) of Brooklyn; Howard Russell (b, 1876) of New Bedford; Charles Abram Russell (b, 1877); Benjamin D. Benson of New York City Faculty of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Mr. and Mrs. Spear, L.M. Johnson, Miss Gibbs, Mr. Feeder, Mr. Davenport Students of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Cora Coulter, Kitty Van Valkenburg, Hannah Durye/Burge, Mary Pettee, Oillie Allston (Chillicothe, O.), Lillie Whitman, Libbie H. Tupper (Hardwick, Mass.), Maggie Cook (Middletown, Conn.), Bell Stowe (Brooklyn), Millie Whitcomb (North Bridgewater, Mass.), Emily Nash (Troy), Sarah Edwards, Bell Morrison, Sarah Edwards (New York City), Emma W. Hubbard (Northampton, Mass.), Fanne Mather, Minnie Dayton, Georgie Masters, Mary Davis, Dotti DeFreest Students at the Naval Academy: S.D. Ames, John P. Wallis, Pat McAllister, C.M. Harris, R. Mitchell, Lieut. Bishop (instructor), E.C. Bartlett, L.S. Shook, H.C. [?Stinson], Clark Smith, Babe Doty, W.C. Strang/Strong.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.181
Scope and Contents
School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1 of 2Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.182
Scope and Contents
School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 2 of 2Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.183
Scope and Contents
Papers read before the Shakespeare ClubDeRidder, Simon17921812184
Scope and Contents
Regimental record book, 1812. Contains orders, muster rolls, and returns. The book had been "Sloop Pollys Account Book," 1792, but only the records inside the front cover remain.4 class capsMU-20721928191 class blazerMU-20731928Photographic Scrapbook1860-195620
Scope and Contents
Retained by donor Wickham, Robert S. Family photograph album, c. 1860-1956. A fine collection of family photos, many of them identified. This album was assembled using rubber cement which has entirely dried up - please use CAUTION when turning pages. ======= Back Log Camp, Sabael, N.Y.1896-195011
Scope and Contents
Pamphlets, 1946-50 and n.d. Includes a history, 1896-1946, a map, 1950 greetings, 1950 general information and rules, and two copies of a much earlier pamphlet, The Back Log Idea.Carpenter, Alice.n.d.12
Scope and Contents
Letter, n.d., to "papa."Carpenter, Alonzo B., Schuylerville.n.d.13
Scope and Contents
Cabinet photograph.Carpenter, Alvin R. I.191414
Scope and Contents
Estate papers, 1914Carpenter, Alvin R. I.191415
Scope and Contents
Letter sent, 1914.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.1914-169516
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914-16 and 1995. Most of the early letters were set by his mother, Mary (Hendy) Carpenter, or his sister, Alice Carpenter, while they were apart due to his parents' separation.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.191717
Scope and Contents
Letters sent, 1917 and n.d. A letter to "Mrs. Landon" written at Oak Street School, 1917, apparently a class exercise; and an undated note to "papa" while he was still with his mother, opening with "I am verry lonesome with out you."Carpenter, Betty, Tulsa, Okla196418
Scope and Contents
Letter sent, 1964, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham; family news.Carpenter family.n.d.19
Scope and Contents
Photograph, unmounted, of Alice and Alvin Carpenter.Carpenter, Mary (Hendy).1914110
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914, on insurance matters.DeRidder family.1905111
Scope and Contents
Genealogical notes; a memo, "Who is Who," by C[atherine] S. DeRidder, Memorial Day, 1905; and a letter, George W. DeRidder, Ballston Spa, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, Binghamton, 15 November 1905.Hendy family.1941112
Scope and Contents
Letter, N.F. McClure, Washington, D.C., to Alvin R. Carpenter, Binghamton, 26 May 1941, enclosing a typescript of a genealogical supplement of the Hendy family of Kentucky.Mayhew, Charles W.1978113
Scope and Contents
Engraving of his residence, Schuylerville, N.Y. It appears, without the full margins, facing p. 260 of Nathaniel B. Sylvester, History of Saratoga County, N.Y. (Philadelphia: Everts and Ensign, 1878).Mayhew, Charles W.1901114
Scope and Contents
Cabinet photograph , inscribed by the sitter to "my dear neice Alice," 1901.Mayhew, Charles W.n.d.115
Scope and Contents
Resolution in memory of CMM, late president, Saratoga Victory Manufacturing Company.Mayhew family.1956-67116
Scope and Contents
Correspondence about the family plot in Prospect Hill Cemetery, Schuylerville, 1956-67, including a plot plan and inscriptions.Saturday Study Club, Schuylerville, N.Y.1901-02117
Scope and Contents
MinutesShakespeare Dramatic Club, Binghamton, N.Y.1956-67118
Scope and Contents
Miscellaneous materials, including "Constitution and By-laws / 19567-1957," along with other materials accumulated by Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, including a newspaper photograph of her as a founding member on its 45th anniversary.Wickham, Alan R.1963-67119
Scope and Contents
Letters sent, 1963-67, to Eloise (Carpenter) Wickham, except for one of 1967 sent to Mr. and Mrs. Alvin R. Carpenter; family news.Wickham, Carolyn.1965120
Scope and Contents
Letter sent to Ruth Everett, 1965.Mayhew family.19011Not Received
Scope and Contents
An issue of The Genealogical Advertiser, vol. 4, no. 1 (March 1901), containing an article, "English Ancestry of Governor Thomas Mayhew."Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).Business papers1947-6521Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.22
Scope and Contents
Cards received.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1966-6723
Scope and Contents
Estate papers, 1966-67. Includes legal papers; an exchange of letters between Conrad and Lucy Meinecke, Springville, N.Y. with Alvin and Betty Carpenter, Binghamton, N,Y, 1967, in reference to Alan Wickham's financial irresponsibility; with a 1961 appraisal of the family residence at 129 Murray Street, including a Polaroid photograph.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).196624
Scope and Contents
"Hospital mail," 1966.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1914-4125
Scope and Contents
Letters received, 1914-41.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1963-6526

Letters received, 1963-65.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1951-6627
Scope and Contents
Medical documents, 1951-66.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1946-6428
Scope and Contents
Newspaper clippings, 1946-64, relating to the Monday Afternoon Club, the Shakespeare Dramatic Club and other theatrical events, politics, etc.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1982-8929
Scope and Contents
School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1982-89210
Scope and Contents
School reports, Schuylerville Union Free School, 1882-89.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1901-02211
Scope and Contents
Papers prepared for the Saturday Study Club, Schuylerville, 1901-02Wickham family, Ballston Spa, N.Y.n.d.212
Scope and Contents
Photograph marked "Bob Wickham's baby sister."Wickham, Robert Sloane.1912213
Scope and Contents
"Biographical Sketch of the National Progressive Party's nominee for Broome County Judge and Surrogate." Pamphlet, n.d. [?1912] 2 pp. Contains a biography of the subject along with useful biographical data on his father, Charles H. Wickham.Wickham, Robert Sloane.1917-58214
Scope and Contents
Financial papers, 1917-58; includes record of income, 1917-25; status, 1956; and utility recapitulation, 1957-58.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.215
Scope and Contents
Law licenses.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.216
Scope and Contents
Medical papers.Wickham, Robert Sloane.1893-96217
Scope and Contents
Pre-academic certificates, Saratoga Springs Union School, 1893-96.Wickham, Robert Sloane.n.d.218
Scope and Contents
Woodcraft materials, clipped from newspapers and magazines.Wickham, Robert Sloane and Eloise (Carpenter).1928-70219
Scope and Contents
Ephemera, 1928-70. Includes church bulletins from Trinity Memorial Church (Episcopal), Binghamton; insurance policies; junk mail; and unclassifiable material. An important item is "My constant prayer," in the hand of RSW.Wickham, Robert Sloane and Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.220
Scope and Contents
Photographs.Business191031Personal191132Personal191233Personal191334Business191335Personal191436Business191437Personal191538Business191539Personal1916310Business1916311Personal1952312Businessn.d.313Bankbooks and other recordsn.d.314Allaben Drawingsn.d.41
Scope and Contents
"Original Sketches of Miss Anne E. Allaben for 'A Saratoga Boyhood' by Robert S. Wickham. (Final proofs of all plates made therefrom opposite.)" Scrapbook with pencil drawings and photo prints pasted in. A 2 pp. carbon copy of a review by Arthur C. Parker of Naples, N,Y. [a prominent Seneca and archaeologist retired from the Rochester Museum of Arts and Sciences] is laid in.Adirondack League Club189351
Scope and Contents
Constitution, by-laws, rules, membership list, annual report, list of allotments with surveys and floor plans. No Carpenter or Mayhew is listed, nor any resident of Saratoga Springs; the club was three years old,An Act, to Organize the Militia of the State of New-York, Passed April 21, 1818. Albany: J.W. Clark181852Amendments to the Militia Law, Passed March 13, 1821. Albany: Websters and Skinners, n,d,182153
Scope and Contents
Marked in ink "Simon DeRidder," along with pp. 7-10 of another copy marked "Walter DeRidder."Annual Register of the United States Naval Academy at Annapolis, Md. 1875-76. Washington: Government Printing Office187554Laws of New-York: viz. An Act, to amend an Act, entitled "an Act to organize the Militia of this State" . . . Albany: J. Buel, [1814]181455
Scope and Contents
Incomplete.Official Register of the Officers and Midshipmen of the United States Naval Academy. Newport, Rhode Island. Nov. 1st, 1864. Newport: Frederick A. Pratt186456
Scope and Contents
The list of midshipmen forming the fourth class of 1864 (p. 23) includes Alvin R. Carpenter from "Texas." He initialed inside front cover, and made notes about the fate of his classmates. A pamphlet, "Regulations Governing the Admission of Candidates into the Naval Academy," is inside the front cover.To the Republican Electors of the State of New-York1814?57
Scope and Contents
Pamphlet, uncutThe Book of Common Prayer. London: Eyre and Spottiswoode190058
Scope and Contents
Pocket format. Inscribed on flyleaf: "Robert S. Wickham / Binghamton / N.Y."Wickham, Robert S., Binghamton, N.Y.n.d.59
Scope and Contents
A Saratoga Boyhood, wrapped and tied for mailing.Wickham, Robert S.June 19985
Scope and Contents
Class of 1928 75th Reunion paperweight for Most Cornell DescendantsWickham, Robert S.19985
Scope and Contents
Class of 1928 65th Reunion medalAnsco Company, Binghamton, N.Y.191561
Scope and Contents
Blueprints, two sheets, 1915.Carpenter, Alvin R. II.1928-4262
Scope and Contents
Certificates, 1928-42 and n.d. Includes a swimming certificate from Cornell, diplomas and certifications, and a tribute upon retirement.DeRidder family.1800's63
Scope and Contents
Papers. Included are two slave indentures, 1827; commencement program, Union College, 1818; two commissions, Walter DeRidder, 1818, and another, 1819; "An Oration on Noses," n,d,; several documents on the division of WDR's estate, 1832; letter, E.H. Dixon, House of Refuge, to Jacob Deridder, "Opposite Schuylervile, "1832, on the occasion of Walter's death; deed, Jesse Chatfield to Kilian DeRidder, 1776; officers' roll of Col. Walter DeRidder's Regiment, New York State Militia; and a small receipt for eggs and silk hose.Mayhew, Abiel W.180964
Scope and Contents
Commission, Columbia County Light Infantry, 1809.Schuylerville Standard.191465
Scope and Contents
Issue of June 11, 1914 with front-page obituary of Alvin R, Carpenter; four copies.Wickham, Alan R.192966
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Binghamton Junior High Schools, 1929.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).188967
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Schuylerville Union School, 1889.Wickham, Robert Sloane.189668
Scope and Contents
Diploma, Saratoga Springs Union Free School, 1896.Carpenter, Charles Mayhew.191271
Scope and Contents
Builder's Estimate and Time Book, 1912. Only one week was used, for 11 March 1912 at 1134 West 37 Drive, apparently in greater Los Angeles. Seven workers' hours are recorded including "CMC" and "Hirashima."DeRidder, C[atherine] S.1887-8872
Scope and Contents
Diary of her European travels "with brother" from December 25, 1877 to July 16, 1878. Slipped in are clipped engravings and clippings, apparently of sites visited: Eaton Hall, Hotel and Pension de l'Ours at Grindelwald, Grand Hotel Pension Bellevue au Lac Zurich, Hotel du Nord, Coeln, and Hotel Monet, Vevey.DeRidder, C[atherine] S.188773
Scope and Contents
Partial copy of the previous item.DeRidder, Walter [?].183574
Scope and Contents
Commonplace book, 1835, with transaction entries of a farmer. Difficult to decipher; commodities include eggs, apples, pork, ham; there is a reference to "Troy and Champlain ofice" and another to "wish to draw wood Cambrge."Mayhew, Ida C., Victory Mills, N.Y.186275
Scope and Contents
Exercise book, Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass., 9 October 1862, later used as a clipping book, with newspaper items dated 1862 to 1873. Inside cover is a photograph inscribed "Maplewood Hotel / Pittsfield / Mass / Formerly Maplewood Institute."Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).1932-3776
Scope and Contents
Account book, 1932-37, of household and personal expenditures at item level. Inside cover is a membership list of a professional women's organization, the "New York Epsilon Athenians – Binghamton, N.Y."Wickham, Robert S.,n.d.77
Scope and Contents
Physics notebook, n.d., probably used at Saratoga Springs High School, but possibly at Cornell.Wickham, Robert S.194878
Scope and Contents
Book of reviews, letters, etc. of A Saratoga Boyhood (1948), including a short errata (p. 4) and a fine letter (p. 5) from Frances Steloff in which she comments that she was the "official flower girl" in Saratoga until she left for Boston in 1900. A press release and an order blank are slipped in."Aunt Fanny." Night Cap Stories. Boston: Estes and Lauriat189581
Scope and Contents
Inscribed inside front cover: "To Alvin / From / Georgina / Merry Xmas / 1914."An Impartial Narrative of the Most Important Engagements Which Took Place Between His Majesty's Forces and the Rebels, During the Irish Rebellion179882
Scope and Contents
First American Edition. Cambridge, N,Y: Tennery and Stockwell, n.d. Inscribed "Simon DeRidder" on p. 3.Luce. S.B. Seamanship. New York: D. Van Nostrand186383
Scope and Contents
Inscribed in ink on flyleaf: "A.R. Carpenter / Midshipman / U.S.N. / 1864." Covers missingRedfield, Henry S. Civil Procedure. Selected Pleadings. No. I. Cornell University College of Lawn.d.84
Scope and Contents
Inscribed on cover: "Robert Sloane Wickham."Class 1864-1868 / United States Naval Academy / The Fiftieth Anniversary1864-186885, 6
Scope and Contents
Pamphlet, two copies.Album1905-1991
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1917-2392
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1923-38101
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Album1939-49102
Scope and Contents
Mounted in traditional oblong albums with black pages, these photos document half a century of hiking, tenting, hunting and fishing in the Adirondacks, along with some domestic views in Schuylerville, and some vacation shots. There is considerable identification: year of shot, place, some people. Some other items such as obituaries are tipped in. The fourth album is primarily of family members.Karl B Stickley1916111

Includes baseball and football games, Asian circus performers, vehicles, and several interiors.Overseas. 8 items.1900-14166

Scope and Contents
Includes Congress Park, streetcar, overturned railroad car, village from above, and a substantial school building.Carte-de-Visite Albumn.d.171
Scope and Contents
Carte de Visite Album, probably of Alvin R. and Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter of Schuylerville, N.Y. Many photographs were identified on the reverse, and these markings have been copied in pencil to the pages themselves, where there were already some captions. See especially four-year-old girl with rifle (p. 13), man with flute (p. 15), boy in Western Union messenger uniform (p.18). Carpenter family members: Meliza (Rich) Carpenter, Alida (Mayhew) Carpenter, Eloise Carpenter, Alvin R. Carpenter, Frank W. Rich Mayhew family members: Emily W. of Detroit, Portia of Detroit DeRidder family members: J.H. DeRidder of Springfield Friends: "son of Enoch Mudge of Boston"; Walter Edward Houghton (b. 1871) of Brooklyn; Howard Russell (b, 1876) of New Bedford; Charles Abram Russell (b, 1877); Benjamin D. Benson of New York City Faculty of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Mr. and Mrs. Spear, L.M. Johnson, Miss Gibbs, Mr. Feeder, Mr. Davenport Students of Maplewood Institute, Pittsfield, Mass.: Cora Coulter, Kitty Van Valkenburg, Hannah Durye/Burge, Mary Pettee, Oillie Allston (Chillicothe, O.), Lillie Whitman, Libbie H. Tupper (Hardwick, Mass.), Maggie Cook (Middletown, Conn.), Bell Stowe (Brooklyn), Millie Whitcomb (North Bridgewater, Mass.), Emily Nash (Troy), Sarah Edwards, Bell Morrison, Sarah Edwards (New York City), Emma W. Hubbard (Northampton, Mass.), Fanne Mather, Minnie Dayton, Georgie Masters, Mary Davis, Dotti DeFreest Students at the Naval Academy: S.D. Ames, John P. Wallis, Pat McAllister, C.M. Harris, R. Mitchell, Lieut. Bishop (instructor), E.C. Bartlett, L.S. Shook, H.C. [?Stinson], Clark Smith, Babe Doty, W.C. Strang/Strong.Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.181
Scope and Contents

Scope and Contents
School exercises, Schuylerville Union Free School, 2 of 2Wickham, Eloise (Carpenter).n.d.183

Regimental record book, 1812. Contains orders, muster rolls, and returns. The book had been "Sloop Pollys Account Book," 1792, but only the records inside the front cover remain.4 class capsMU-20721928191 class blazerMU-20731928Photographic Scrapbook1860-195620
Scope and Contents
Retained by donor Wickham, Robert S. Family photograph album, c. 1860-1956. A fine collection of family photos, many of them identified. This album was assembled using rubber cement which has entirely dried up - please use CAUTION when turning pages. >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
